message:
fix: blocking pool task loss, Count repoll panic, timer waker isolation

Three fail-closed hardening fixes:

1. runtime/blocking_pool.rs — When a retiring thread could not
   un-retire (max active threads reached) but a task was waiting in
   the queue, the task was silently dropped.  Fix: force un-retire
   and increment active_threads when a queued task exists, ensuring
   no task is ever lost due to the thread count cap.

2. stream/count.rs — Polling a Count future after completion
   previously returned a cached value silently, masking logic errors
   in callers that polled a resolved future.  Fix: panic with a
   descriptive assertion ("polled after completion") to fail fast.
   Tests updated to use catch_unwind and verify the panic message.

3. time/driver.rs — A panicking waker during timer expiry would
   unwind through the driver loop, preventing remaining wakers from
   being fired.  Fix: wrap each waker invocation in catch_unwind so
   a single bad waker cannot block other timers from firing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
